title: Skill-Mine — Self-Extending Governed Skill Library
note: PRD body superseded by the 12 Required PRD Corrections in plan.md where the two conflict.
goal: A manual, gated, phased system where the harness mines its own successful runs into executable OpenCode skills — quarantined candidates, behavioral validation, atomic promotion, catalog budget, manual retirement. No vector DB, no training, no autonomous trigger (deferred).
research_basis: arXiv 2026 (3 parallel scout scans: self-improving agents, skill acquisition, long-horizon memory) + read-only review audit (5 blockers, 4 medium risks). Key papers: 2607.13091, 2607.08010, 2605.18284, 2607.14159, 2605.22148, 2607.09711, 2607.12790, 2607.13683.

success_criteria.check: Sanitized capture: /skill-mine <sha> produces a trace with changed-paths + verify-commands + exit-codes and NO raw user messages, NO tool output, NO secret-shaped strings
success_criteria.verify: bash .opencode/tool/verify.sh exit 0; rg -n 'password|secret|token|apiKey|credential' .opencode/.skill-mine/traces/*.json → no matches; trace JSON has changed_paths, verify_commands, exit_codes, status keys

success_criteria.check: Validate gate rejects invalid candidates with a specific reason; accepts known-good skills
Candidates quarantined outside .opencode/skill/ (not advertised to model before promotion)
success_criteria.verify: test ! -e .opencode/skill/<candidate-name>; opencode debug skill --pure 2>/dev/null | rg <candidate-name> → no match (restart-gated)

success_criteria.check: Atomic gated promotion: promote without validate → rejected; after validate passes → skill loads
success_criteria.verify: promote broken candidate → exit 1, candidate intact; promote validated candidate → exit 0, .opencode/skill/<name>/SKILL.md exists, candidate dir empty, bash .opencode/tool/verify.sh exit 0

success_criteria.check: Catalog budget enforced: surfaced mined skills ≤ cap (default 10); exceeding → promotion rejected
success_criteria.verify: rg -n 'mined' .opencode/skill/*/SKILL.md | wc -l ≤ cap; promote beyond cap → exit 1 with budget message
git check-ignore .opencode/.skill-mine/traces/test → prints path (ignored); git status --porcelain .opencode/.skill-mine/ → clean

success_criteria.check: Sanitized provenance in promoted skills: metadata has source_commit, mined_date, judge_version, scope, evidence_summary; no raw transcript body
success_criteria.verify: rg -n 'source_commit|mined_date|judge_version|scope' .opencode/skill/<mined>/SKILL.md → matches; no <session/<user/raw tool-output blocks in body

tasks.title: Create quarantine structure + gitignore + sync exclusion
tasks.end_state: .opencode/.skill-mine/{traces,candidates,archive}/ + state.json (catalog budget 10, judge v1, scope project) + candidates/.gitkeep exist; .skill-mine/ gitignored + sync-excluded
tasks.verify: git check-ignore .opencode/.skill-mine/traces/test → ignored; bash .opencode/tool/sync-template.sh && test ! -e template/.opencode/.skill-mine → exit 0; bash .opencode/tool/verify.sh → exit 0

tasks.title: Create skill-mine-validate gate
tasks.end_state: skill-mine-validate tool checks frontmatter schema, uniqueness, provenance, helper syntax, behavioral RED/GREEN/REFACTOR; exits 1 on failure with reason, 0 on pass
tasks.verify: bash .opencode/tool/skill-mine-validate .opencode/skill/memory/ → exit 0; bash .opencode/tool/skill-mine-validate .opencode/.skill-mine/candidates/broken/ → exit 1 with reason; bash .opencode/tool/verify.sh → exit 0

tasks.title: Create /skill-mine command (capture)
tasks.end_state: /skill-mine capture <sha|range> produces sanitized JSON trace in .skill-mine/traces/ with 7 keys, secret-scanned, no raw user messages/tool output/diffs
tasks.verify: /skill-mine capture <recent-sha> → trace exists; rg -n 'password|secret|token|apiKey|credential' .opencode/.skill-mine/traces/*.json → no matches; trace has 7 keys; bash .opencode/tool/verify.sh → exit 0

tasks.title: Create /skill-mine distill (candidate generation)
tasks.end_state: /skill-mine distill <trace> produces a candidate in .skill-mine/candidates/<name>/SKILL.md + helpers with provenance metadata; NOT loaded by OpenCode (quarantine)
tasks.verify: /skill-mine distill <trace> → candidate exists with valid frontmatter + provenance; test ! -e .opencode/skill/<candidate-name>; bash .opencode/tool/skill-mine-validate candidates/<name>/ → passes deterministic checks; bash .opencode/tool/verify.sh → exit 0

tasks.title: Create /skill-mine promote (atomic gated move)
tasks.end_state: /skill-mine promote <candidate> runs validate (full incl behavioral); if pass: atomic move candidates/ → .opencode/skill/<name>/, commit sanitized provenance, update catalog count; if fail: reject with reason; if budget exceeded: reject
tasks.verify: promote broken candidate → exit 1, candidate intact; promote validated candidate → exit 0, test -e .opencode/skill/<name>/SKILL.md, test ! -e .opencode/.skill-mine/candidates/<name>/; rg provenance keys in SKILL.md → matches; bash .opencode/tool/verify.sh → exit 0

tasks.title: Reuse telemetry
tasks.end_state: skill invocations logged to .skill-mine/usage.jsonl (gitignored); /skill-mine usage reports per-skill invocation count + last-used date
tasks.verify: invoke promoted skill → test -s .opencode/.skill-mine/usage.jsonl; /skill-mine usage → shows skill count ≥1; git check-ignore .opencode/.skill-mine/usage.jsonl → ignored; bash .opencode/tool/verify.sh → exit 0

tasks.title: Create /skill-mine gc (manual retirement)
tasks.end_state: /skill-mine gc re-validates promoted mined skills + checks reuse; fails re-validation OR no reuse → move to .skill-mine/archive/, commit deletion; manual, on-demand
tasks.verify: /skill-mine gc with stale skill → test ! -e .opencode/skill/<stale-name>, test -e .opencode/.skill-mine/archive/<stale-name>/SKILL.md; bash .opencode/tool/verify.sh → exit 0

tasks.title: Document deferred automation (no code)
tasks.end_state: skill-mine.md + MEMORY.md document: autonomous capture deferred (no reliable completion marker); trigger for automation = completion marker emerges AND ≥3 promoted skills with reuse
tasks.verify: rg -n 'deferred|assisted|autonomous' .opencode/command/skill-mine.md → matches deferral note; bash .opencode/tool/verify.sh → exit 0

open_questions.question: Is skill-mine-validate a shell script or a bun .ts file?
open_questions.resolution: Decide at /plan based on gate complexity. Shell is simpler (no compile gate); bun .ts gets verify.sh typecheck coverage.

open_questions.question: Does the behavioral gate fully automate or prompt the build agent?
open_questions.resolution: Prompt the build agent — writing-skills contract is a manual pressure test; full automation needs a judge model + held-out scenarios (out of scope for personal use).

open_questions.question: Should mined skills be versioned (v1, v2) when re-distilled?
open_questions.resolution: Defer — git history IS versioning; re-distill produces a new candidate, promote replaces + archives the old. Decide at /plan.

open_questions.question: Should the catalog budget be per-agent (different caps for build vs plan)?
open_questions.resolution: Default a global cap (10); per-agent is a future refinement. Decide at /plan.

open_questions.question: Does /skill-mine need a plugin for usage telemetry, or can the command log it?
open_questions.resolution: The command logs it — skill invocations are visible in the session log. No plugin needed (keeps sole-writer invariant).

stop_conditions: skill-mine-validate cannot reliably check behavioral value (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR too expensive or unreliable) → stop, surface to user; may need a judge model or human-in-the-loop
Catalog budget too restrictive (10 too low for real use) → widen with evidence
Distilled skills consistently fail validation (low distillation quality) → stop, investigate the distill prompt or the trace quality; do not lower the gate
Secret leakage detected in a committed promoted skill → stop, audit all promoted skills, add a stronger secret-scan gate
non_goals: Autonomous capture triggers (no reliable post-ship hook; deferred to Phase 4)
Vector/embedding retrieval (local files + description-based surfacing sufficient)
skills.urls remote publishing (overkill for personal use)
Plugin-based skill writes (build agent is sole writer)
Automated retirement (manual /skill-mine gc from day 1; automation deferred)
Strictness migration, plugin/sdk/ (existing deferred items, unchanged)
